message:
feat(algorithms): add load centrality, closeness vitality, k-core family + conformance fixtures

Expands the `fnx-algorithms` crate with a set of NetworkX parity additions
and registers matching strict conformance fixtures in `fnx-conformance`,
plus Python-binding plumbing in `fnx-python`. The E2E oracle contract is
regenerated to reflect the new fixture inventory (94 -> 106).

New algorithm surfaces in fnx-algorithms::
- `load_centrality` / `load_centrality_directed` / `load_centrality_normalized`
  with a dedicated `LoadCentralityResult` carrying per-node scores and a
  `ComplexityWitness`. Uses BFS-based shortest-path accumulation matching
  `networkx.load_centrality` semantics.
- `ClosenessVitalityResult` and associated entry points for closeness
  vitality on weighted/unweighted graphs.
- `KCoreResult` shared across the k-core / k-shell / k-crust / k-corona
  decomposition functions, returning both retained nodes and edges so
  downstream consumers can reconstruct the induced subgraph.

Conformance coverage:
- New strict fixtures under `crates/fnx-conformance/fixtures/generated/`
  covering the twelve scenarios listed in the oracle contract update
  (bipartite_triangle, bridges_chain, closeness_vitality_path,
  clustering_square, density_triangle, global_node_connectivity_k4,
  is_eulerian_k4, k_core_triangle_pendant, mst_kruskal_triangle,
  number_components_two, shortest_path_length_chain, voterank_star).
- `fnx-conformance/src/lib.rs` registers the new oracle functions so the
  strict harness can dispatch each fixture to the correct Rust entry point.

Python bindings:
- `fnx-python/src/algorithms.rs` exposes the new entry points so the
  PyO3 layer is in sync with the Rust surface.

Oracle contract:
- `artifacts/e2e/v1/e2e_scenario_matrix_oracle_contract_v1.json` now
  lists 106 fixtures (was 94); 22 remain uncovered pending follow-up
  strict-harness work, vs the prior 8.
